definition: The explicit statement of intent a signer must accept before a signature commits — the electronic-signature equivalent of "I intend to sign and agree to be bound by this document." Captured as a boolean on the Signature Record; the signature-pad block refuses to emit a sign event until it is affirmed, making deliberate intent a structural precondition of capture.
usage: The affirmation text is author-supplied (the affirmation attribute) so each document can state what is being agreed to; the block owns only the gate that intent must precede signature.
